programme.desc: Tia and M2 prepare a pitbull for its forever home, only for the adoption to take an unexpected turn. A new parolee comes to Villalobos from Boston

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a couple afraid to have a child due to their hostile cat, and tries to make peace between two felines who scrap around their kitty litter

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson helps a good cat stand up to its two feline attackers. He also meets a couple fed up with their cats eating everything in sight

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: During a routine hunting licence check, Game Wardens Ben and Chelsea discover illegal drugs. And, trespassers get caught in a dangerous restricted area

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet

programme.desc: Pete Nelson and the team give a thrill-seeking couple their dream treehouse - straddling three cedars with a wraparound deck providing 360 degrees of stunning views

programme.title: Gator Boys

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns hatch a plan to deal with a lumber shortage, but then a sudden injury threatens to split the family apart

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Rugged Justice
programme.desc: Washington State's fish and wildlife police bear dog unit forces a problem bear away from a family's home. Plus, an unstable woman roams the interstate. Documentary series following the work of the Washington Department of Fish and Wildlife Law Enforcement Programme

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy is in Philadelphia to help a cat named Charlee rebuild the confidence that her feline sister Frankie has nearly beaten out of her

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The anglers travel to Milne Day off the eastern tip of Papua New Guinea, the rumoured home to huge schools of the giant trevally. The crew try to hook one on a fly rod

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Wayde King tries to keep his ATM team motivated by sending them to a boot camp in the hope they work together to pull off the creation of two challenging salt water tanks

programme.title: Ken Loach Remembers - Up the Junction
programme.desc: Ken Loach on Up the Junction, his landmark 1965 contribution to the BBC's The Wednesday Play strand, adapted from Nell Dunn's celebrated stories of working-class life in Battersea. Blending drama with documentary realism, the production shocked audiences with its frank depiction of sex, illegal abortion and everyday poverty, attracting record complaints while redefining television drama in Britain

programme.title: Up the Junction: The Wednesday Play
programme.desc: A well-to-do girl leaves her parents and comfortable home in fashionable Chelsea to sample life in working-class Battersea, but is not prepared for the culture shock awaiting her. Drama, starring Carol White, Geraldine Sherman and Vickery Turner. Directed by Ken Loach

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team examines the threat of space weather, meeting some of the dedicated people working to keep the Earth safe from the very worst that the Sun can throw at it. Chris Lintott meets Jim Wild, the president of the Royal Astronomical Society, learn about the Carrington Event of 1859, when a massive solar storm caused aurorae as far south as the Caribbean. Maggie Aderin drops into Warwick University to discover how Dr Ravindra Desai's research is helping people grasp how the matter spat out by the sun interacts with the Earth

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Chessmen v Mathematicians: The Competition for Third Place
programme.desc: Three chess players challenge the Mathematicians in a bid to win third place in the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced people's understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment. But perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz concludes, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Rugby Boys: The Final
programme.desc: Rugby players compete against a trio from the Cambridge Quiz Society in the final of the quiz show where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox begins his epic exploration of the cosmos, beginning with the great luminous bodies that bring light and warmth to the universe, the stars. Throughout the opening episode, he looks at how stars created life and meaning in the cosmos, from the dawn of time through to the arrival of our own star, the Sun

programme.title: Laurence Rees Remembers - The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: Historical filmmaker Laurence Rees looks back on his acclaimed 1997 documentary series, explaining its origins and how the fall of the Berlin Wall opened possibilities for interviews with people who previously had been unreachable

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: Account of Hitler's rise to power, featuring archive film from Russia and eyewitness accounts by former Nazi party members. The first programme concentrates on the Nazi party's formation after the First World War, the growth of support in the late 1920s and the eventual Fuehrer's appointment as Chancellor in 1933

programme.title: Ken Loach Remembers - Old Oak
programme.desc: Ken Loach reflects on The Old Oak, his deeply moving portrait of solidarity, displacement and community in a former mining village in County Durham. Set against the arrival of Syrian refugees in a town scarred by austerity and deindustrialisation, the film explores whether compassion and collective action can survive in fractured times

programme.title: The Old Oak (2023)
programme.desc: TJ Ballantyne was born and bred in his village in Country Durham and he has never left the close-knit community, becoming a valued leader following the 1984 miners strikes. He owns The Old Oak, the last remaining pub. The arrival of a bus of Syrian refugees exposes division and despair, leading to an ugly confrontation between 20-something new arrival Yara and a local man. TJ risks the wrath of old friend Charlie to help Yara. Ken Loach's drama, starring Dave Turner

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Clint Eastwood - Part Two: American Filmmaker
programme.desc: Part two of two. Profile of Clint Eastwood, examining his lifelong commitment to the process of film-making. The actor made his directorial debut with the 1971 thriller Play Misty for Me, which proved the foundation for a career encompassing classics such as The Outlaw Josey Wales, Bird and Unforgiven. With contributions from Gene Hackman, Meryl Streep and Dina Ruiz

programme.title: This Cultural Life
programme.desc: Director Ken Loach talks to John Wilson about the key moments in his life that helped shaped his creativity. From his Midlands childhood as the son of a factory worker and annual summer holidays in Blackpool to his love for Czech cinema, which would become a huge inspiration on his own career. He also discusses his films for television, including Up the Junction and Cathy Come Home

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Rugby Boys: The Final
programme.desc: Rugby players compete against a trio from the Cambridge Quiz Society in the final of the quiz show where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren Mitchell presents

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox begins his epic exploration of the cosmos, beginning with the great luminous bodies that bring light and warmth to the universe, the stars. Throughout the opening episode, he looks at how stars created life and meaning in the cosmos, from the dawn of time through to the arrival of our own star, the Sun

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz concludes, with two teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure the last place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Three civil servants with a shared love of Radio 4's The Archers pit their wits against a trio of music lovers to make connections between things that initially do not appear to be linked. Victoria Coren presents

programme.title: The Making of King Arthur
programme.desc: Poet Simon Armitage traces the evolution of the Arthurian legend through the literature of the medieval age, revealing that King Arthur is not the great national hero he is usually considered to be. He is a fickle and transitory character who was appropriated by the Normans to justify their conquest; he was cuckolded when French writers began adapting the story; and it took Thomas Malory's masterpiece of English literature, Le Mort d'Arthur, to restore his dignity and reclaim him as the national hero known today

programme.title: Unforgiven (1992)
programme.desc: Two notorious gunslingers come out of retirement to help a young assassin collect the $1,000 bounty on the heads of a pair of cowboys who attacked and mutilated a prostitute in the backwoods town of Big Whiskey. The local sheriff, however, has no intention of letting the pair impinge on his own authority. Oscar-winning Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, with Gene Hackman, Morgan Freeman, Jaimz Woolvett and Richard Harris

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Clint Eastwood - Part One: Out of the West
programme.desc: Part one of two. Profile of Hollywood icon Clint Eastwood, focusing on his poverty-stricken childhood in Thirties California, through early television success in Rawhide, to classic roles in films, including A Fistful of Dollars. With contributions from luminaries, such as Rip Torn, Eli Wallach and directors Don Siegel, Sergio Leone and Martin Scorsese

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on December 17, 1999. Featuring Tom Jones and Cerys Matthews, B*Witched and Ladysmith Black Mambazo, Progress Presents The Boy Wunda, Vengaboys, Artful Dodger feat Craig David and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Tony Blackburn looks at the weekly pop chart from 1977 and introduces Osibisa, the Wurzels, Neil Innes, Demis Roussos, Bob Marley and the Wailers, ELO, Frankie Miller, the Stranglers, Honky and Rod Stewart, with a dance sequence from Legs & Co

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Exploration of the cultural impact of the USA's spectacular landscape, revealing how although the country had no ruins or ancient monuments, natural wonders could be found almost anywhere. Presenter Robert Hughes shows how the rise of the railroad opened up a whole new world of discovery while at the same time inaugurating America's industrial future

programme.title: The Making of King Arthur
programme.desc: Poet Simon Armitage traces the evolution of the Arthurian legend through the literature of the medieval age, revealing that King Arthur is not the great national hero he is usually considered to be. He is a fickle and transitory character who was appropriated by the Normans to justify their conquest; he was cuckolded when French writers began adapting the story; and it took Thomas Malory's masterpiece of English literature, Le Mort d'Arthur, to restore his dignity and reclaim him as the national hero known today

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Phil returns home to find someone hanging around his house. When he checks his doorbell footage, he sees the burglar drop some incriminating evidence

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Well Worth the Wait in Cornwall
programme.desc: An old Sunday School in Cornwall undergoes a transformation and two unique properties in Hampshire and Lancashire are up for auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian hosts a special show all about tea and the history of tea-drinking. In Eastbourne, he is serenaded by organist Chris Mannion as they reminisce about tea dances, while in Hastings Danny is given a 'tea-tastic' masterclass by etiquette expert Rebecca Gildea. At the end of the show, he counts down the most profitable tea-related antiques and collectibles

programme.desc: Officers find a haul of suspected cannabis plants is found at a residential home, stolen bike parts are discovered in a derelict hotel, and dogs sniff out drugs in three bags coming in on a flight from New Jersey at the airport

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson takes fellow actor Jo Joyner horse riding near the small town of Wooler as he introduces her to his beloved Northumberland. The Cheviots provide a stunning backdrop at the riding centre at Kimmerton - where Jo gets back in the saddle for the first time in years. With her love of riding renewed, Robson takes Jo to Heatherslaw Mill for an outdoor cookery lesson to make soda bread

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a potential buyer to find a home in the Lake District, presenting cottages in some of the region's most sought-after villages, including a unique mystery house

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Joe Swift are in Wilmslow to help Vin and Reena transform their chaotic patch of grass, gravel and concrete into a zen and grow-your-own garden for family healing with a budget of £4,000

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Ross Kemp in which contestants compete for cash by crossing a bridge made up of stepping stones, each of which has a different option. Friends Mark, Sheila, Giselle and Shaun must try to get from A to B by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies in order to cross the studio floor safely, or risk falling off the bridge and out of the game

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Ria Lina

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a life-sized model horse used as a teaching aid at a riding school for the disabled, a chair that survived the Second World War, a handmade globe that reveals what time it is anywhere around the world and a leather darts case

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda hosts a prom pre-party at her flat for Georgie and her friends, but Felicity gets in the way, the make-up designer is terrible and the limo gets stuck

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: The diggers move in at the allotments, forcing the protest group to take extreme action. With time running out for Richard, he decides to lay it all on the line for Emily - but will it prove enough?

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the USA Changed Soccer
programme.desc: How soccer in the USA would experience a second coming, with the likes of David Beckham heading to States, after the boom and bust of the 1980s. By the late 1970s, the NASL was continuing to attract the biggest footballing stars in the world, with new innovations making the sport attractive to its American audience. However, the good times didn't last, and the NASL was in a dire financial situation by the mid-1980s, seeing the end of the league, as the money and interest dried up. However, the college system helped fuel the rise of the US Women's National Team, whose World Cup and Olympic triumphs transformed women's football globally, while Beckham's arrival in MLS in 2007 reignited the men's game before Lionel Messi took Inter Miami and MLS to new heights in 2023

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Julian Clary, Sophie Willan, Harry Pinero, Liza Tarbuck, Judi Love and Tom Allen

programme.desc: Miss Skerrit has an aggressive detainee in custody, detective Akle is taking care of a deceased loved one, and the mystery of an abandoned crashed car is solved

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson, his brother David and uncle Matheson head to the Scottish Highlands to take a pack horse camping trip. With a guide leading Maggie the horse - who's carrying the kit - the Greens enjoy their scenic hike in the East Cairngorms. The family take a woodworking lesson in the forest, and fish in scenic Loch an Eilein

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a buyer to return to her childhood home county of Devon, and visits an organic orchard producing award-winning cider

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock revisits a garden in Northampton that was designed to be accessible for a couple's disabled children. Plus, what became of a theatrical-themed garden in Gosport and a plot in Nottingham inspired by the Lost Gardens of Heligan

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Ross Kemp in which contestants compete for cash by crossing a bridge made up of stepping stones, each of which has a different option. programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Joe Swash and George Clarke join Lauren Laverne and Vernon Kay on the sofa. Plus, global superstar Zayn Malik delivers this week's One Big Thank You

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos erupts at the Vic, Chelsea's world is turned upside down, and George and Gina try to clear the air

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.sub-title: Is It a Bedroom, Or Is It a Garage?
programme.desc: The team help the Jamiesons from East Sussex transform the home that's been in their family for 40 years. They strip every item from the converted bungalow and display them all in a warehouse where they discover 106 animal ornaments, 87 board games and over 1,000 pieces of china. As they sort through the decades of keepsakes, mum Nadine reflects on her childhood and the challenges of raising her daughters

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: At Drylaw Police Station in Edinburgh, Inspector Alison Lawrie investigates when a call comes in reporting vulnerable 18-year-old Taylor missing. Officers visit his adoptive parents' house to find out whether they have seen him but he hasn't answered any calls or messages from anyone and their concern grows when they learn Taylor has recently started transitioning from female to male. In the south of the city, Sergeant McMinn is on duty when a report that 13-year-old Ava has failed to return to her care unit after school comes in and alarm bells ring when he discovers she has no phone on her

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
programme.desc: A look at the careers of Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, widely regarded as the finest players of their generation, and long-time rivals at Barcelona and Real Madrid respectively. The two players have won the Ballon d'or 13 times between them, with Messi winning eight of them, and they both head to this World Cup for what is likely to be the final time

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Alex gets too competitive at laser tag, Steve and Serena go public, Ron finds a Space Invaders machine, and Joyce overdresses when she misunderstands what Laser Lounge means

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes quiz, with Olympian Greg Rutherford, comedian and actor Sanjeev Kohli, presenter and writer Janet Ellis and actor Louisa Lytton playing to win money for their chosen charities. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money with each correct answer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: When Chelsea goes to make a coffee, she notices her milk hasn't been delivered. She reviews her doorbell footage and is shocked to discover a man breaking into her house all while she was asleep upstairs. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan shows how even fancy apartments are targets for thieves. And Anabela is gobsmacked when she discovers her cat has been stolen, but will turning to social media manage to recover him?

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam examines his relationship with drugs after he finds himself doing cocaine at a warehouse party with a stranger on a Tuesday night, when he should be preparing for his mental health assessment the next day

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam lies to Jess about missing his mental-health assessment and tensions in their relationship reach breaking point, leaving him suddenly nostalgic for the simplicity of first love

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Liz's disappearance has left an empty space at the gym which Andy and Jay compete to fill - and with his body positivity classes struggling and his relationship drifting, Andy cannot afford to lose. Comedy, starring Allan Mustafa

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy starts his new job at a different gym but is soon spooked by high-energy fitness influencer Kara's talk of high standards. What will become of his long-distance bromance with Spooner?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A short comedy film about sleep deprivation, the brutality of the music industry and the power of Iggy Pop's hip thrust

programme.title: Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The reporter investigates the controversial religious organisation, but finds himself denied access to anyone involved. Adopting a different approach, he resorts to recruiting actors to re-enact the personal stories of former members - only to find his project is under surveillance by representatives of the church

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: The Bislett Games. Coverage of the sixth meeting of the campaign, held at Bislett Stadium in Oslo, Norway. George Mills set a new British record of 12:46.59 in the 5,000m here last year, but still had to settle for fourth place behind American victor Nico Young and Ethiopians Biniam Mehary and Kuma Girma, There was also a win for Young's compatriot Isabella Whittaker in the 400m, who beat home favourite Henriette Jaeger into second place, with Team GB's Amber Anning third and Ireland's Rhasidat Adeleke fourth

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions (2021)
programme.desc: The survivors of a deadly game designed by a sinister corporation are dragged back into an even deadlier trap. And as a handful of former escapees join in, the competitors must work together to outsmart their inhuman tormentors and give their all to solve increasingly sophisticated puzzles. Horror sequel, starring Taylor Russell and Logan Miller

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is back at his parents' house in Leeds intending to prepare for a performance at a local spoken-word night, but instead procrastinates and plays old video games. But when he turns on his childhood computer and stumbles on an old home-made hip-hop track he created with his teenage mates, it triggers a memory

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar's investigation reveals a white supremacist that was campaigning outside the Suites hotel ahead of the protest may have influenced the riot. He also heads further afield to Scampton, Lincolnshire where residents have been campaigning against the Home Office's planned asylum centre in the former RAF base

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three hosts are each given a budget of £5,000 to find a car that can hold its own on a racetrack and handle everyday tasks, before testing their purchases in Germany. James May puts two Porsches through their paces on the Top Gear test circuit, and former Downing Street communications director Alastair Campbell tries out the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two emotionally unassembled young men start a play group, Funboys Fun Club, for childish young men to engage in some wholesome mucking about. When they come across severely depressed 60-year-old Marty, they try their best to bring some joy back to his life. Pilot for the sitcom, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Early

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor return to the abandoned manor hoping for answers but the arrival of two mysterious figures leads to a dangerous chase. The verdict is given in court sooner than anyone expected and at school, Pip loses control earning herself a suspension. A crucial clue cracks the case wide open

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip, Ravi and Connor form a plan that might finally bring their hunt for Jamie to an end, but first Pip must swallow her pride and ask Cara for help. In a daring breaking-and-entering mission, Pip faces the person behind Jamie's disappearance and when a complicated truth emerges, she's forced to accept the line between good an evil is a murky one

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the work of people striving to protect Asia's endangered wildlife, and what routes conservation could take in the future. In Japan, scientists take pregnant sharks, killed accidentally in fishing nets, and rescue their unborn young in an incubating system that simulates a womb. The programme also visits a village that used to be a hotbed of poaching and is now a centre for bird tourism

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The two finalists head to Longleat Estate to transform rental cottages, taking inspiration from either the nearby safari park or from the surrounding countryside. They aim to show judges Michelle Ogundehin and Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen how much their design ability has evolved during the competition before one of them wins Interior Design Masters and a partnership with retailer Next for their own homewares range

programme.title: Blade Runner 2049 (2017)
programme.desc: An android-hunter working for the Los Angeles Police Department discovers a long-buried secret, which leads him to track down former detective Rick Deckard, who's been missing for 30 years and is linked to the case. Denis Villeneuve's sci-fi thriller, the sequel to Ridley Scott's 1982 classic, starring Ryan Gosling, Harrison Ford, Ana de Armas and Sylvia Hoeks

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar visits Kirkby, Merseyside to find out how a peaceful protest outside a hotel housing asylum seekers turned into a riot in February 2023 and uncovers the blueprint for a national wave of violent riots that took place across the country in the summer of 2024. Mobeen is soon confronted with serious allegations about what a group of asylum seekers have supposedly been up to in the small town, and attempts to uncover the truth but locals close ranks and warn each other against speaking to him, claiming Mobeen and the film crew will twist their words to make them look bad

programme.title: Planet Sex with Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Cara examines the idea of gender, what it is and why it matters, confronting her own gender fluidity and masculinity along the way. She explores drag with a trans man in LA, discovers a third gender culture in Mexico and experiences a 'gender swap' in Spain

programme.title: America's New Female Right
programme.desc: In America, a new wave of young women who are championing extreme views on social media, including content that is increasingly shocking and - surprisingly - antifeminist. Journalist Layla Wright travels from her home town in Liverpool to meet influencers at the forefront of a new movement on the political right who use highly inflammatory language

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: The search for the gang of thieves known for stalking celebrities on social media and targeting those with expensive watches. Journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones' £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the work of people striving to protect Asia's endangered wildlife, and what routes conservation could take in the future. In Japan, scientists take pregnant sharks, killed accidentally in fishing nets, and rescue their unborn young in an incubating system that simulates a womb. The programme also visits a village that used to be a hotbed of poaching and is now a centre for bird tourism

programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh
programme.desc: Autumn continues, with apples being harvested just before unprecedented flooding wreaks havoc, while clear skies bring celestial wonders for the astronomers

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The finalists jet off to the Caribbean where they are set three challenges designed to broaden their culinary horizons like never before. For starters, they prepare a barbecue feast for locals, before having a shot at local fine dining at the Estate house within the five-star Jumby Bay Island Resort, with each of them preparing a dish from the dinner menu. They finish off with another task at Jumby Bay - working as private chefs for a VIP dinner, where guests include cricketing legend Viv Richards

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: The pressure builds as chicks prepare to fledge and predators close in. The team hope to capture even more intimate moments from the lives of the wildlife calling National Trust Crom home through their 24-hour remote camera surveillance. Away from Crom, Iolo Williams spends his final night live at Knepp, where rewilding is transforming the landscape

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove his patient's voice box to save his life, while another takes on a life-changing procedure for a patient with a rare auto-immune condition

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Willams helps an air ambulance pilot to find a rural home that is within an hour of Belfast airport, and also has a go at Irish dancing by Lough Neagh

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill travel to Coventry to help mother-and-daughter nurses Barbara and Tate transform their completely empty garden into an exotic jungle retreat

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Japanese Alps: Nagano to Narai Juku
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ends the first leg of his journey on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps. In the town of Obuse, Michael learns how Japan's most famous artwork, Hokusai's The Great Wave off Kanagawa, was made. He visits the largest wasabi farm in the world, and heads to the historic mountain town of Narai Juku, with its beautifully preserved wooden houses

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, Michael Portillo travels to South Korea's south west, where food, protest and landscape reveal the values that have shaped the modern nation. Arriving by high-speed train in Gwangju, he explores the region's deep-rooted food culture. Later, the presenter confronts a darker chapter in South Korea's history at the May 18th Memorial Park. Leaving the mainland, Michael crosses by ferry to Jeju Island, a volcanic landscape dominated by Mount Halla

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The HSBC Championships. Coverage of day three of the women's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring singles matches from the second round. The top four seeds enter the competition at this stage, including the top-ranked Jessica Pegula, and her American compatriot Amanda Anisimova, who was runner-up both here and at Wimbledon last year. The other two who received byes are talented Canadian teenager Victoria Mboko and Olympic gold medallist Belinda Bencic of Switzerland

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure reaches the Dalmatian coastal city of Zadar, once an important Venetian trading hub and still a centre for the production of Maraschino cherry liqueur, a Venetian delicacy. He visits a renowned tailor to try on a traditional Dalmatian suit in the medieval backstreets of Trogir. At the Cathedral of St Domnius, Michael learns about the importance of Catholicism to the Croatian people

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson explores more villages around the UK, starting with Runswick Bay in North Yorkshire, where he discovers how this popular tourist site was once a place of danger. Benvisits the caves in the sea cliffs known as hob-holes to uncover how they once helped supply a thriving jet industry and finds evidence of how, in the late-1800s, the village became the inspiration for a group of artists who became known as the British Impressionists

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Investigating whether revolutionary technology can transform the lives of millions of disabled people, with a look at coffee making robots in Japan and bionic limbs in New York

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Refrigerators have been quietly 'keeping things cool' in the kitchen for over 100 years, and yet most people barely give them a second thought. Hannah Fry visits the production line at Bosch to find the lifeblood at the heart of every fridge, and gets a lesson in carrot droop

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan celebrate the season's standout wildlife moments. Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the seabird colonies at Bempton Cliffs, where thousands of gannets, guillemots and puffins crowd the towering coastline

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon has one chance to tackle a life-threatening tumour in his patient's liver and diaphragm. A neurosurgeon must save her patient from paralysis with unerring precision

programme.title: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Investigating the crime happening in plain sight on high streets across Britain - from illegal cigarettes hidden in the back room of a minimart in Rochdale to cocaine being bought over the counter from a shop in the West Midlands. Also, exposing the crime gangs exploiting the vulnerable and setting up shop in Britain

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Lyndsey Telford hears from former Church of Ireland workers as they reveal concerns over the handling of safeguarding cases

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBC's world affairs editor reflects on the week's major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: The Heart of Me (2003)
programme.desc: A bohemian artist in 1930s England falls for her handsome brother-in-law - and when he makes his illicit intentions clear, they begin a passionate affair, unaware the eventual discovery of their betrayal will lead to trauma and tragedy. Period drama based on Rosamond Lehmann's novel, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Paul Bettany, Olivia Williams and Eleanor Bron

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Exploring nuclear fusion - the holy grail for limitless clean energy - and whether it will come soon enough to avoid the worst of the climate crisis

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton see if Nitin Ganatra, Rev Kate Bottley and Gladiator Apollo can sort fact from fiction. Today's adventure takes them to Bristol

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: On patrol in Belfast city centre Adam pursues a fleeing suspect on foot. A few minutes later, he must do it again, as the man bursts out of the police car, trying to escape. Back at the station, Anna and Perri can't resist the temptation to hide the boss's car, temporarily. Meanwhile, the whole team leap into action when a stolen car fails to stop

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps Lisa swap Northampton for a Powys life reboot with her children and takes to a canoe, 128 feet above the ground

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Joe Swift are in Wilmslow to help Vin and Reena transform their chaotic patch of grass, gravel and concrete into a zen and grow-your-own garden for family healing with a budget of £4,000

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Roses are stealing the spotlight at Longmeadow, and Monty Don is celebrating all the full, head-turning bloom. Ashley Edwards visits Benton End in Suffolk, a place of artistic vision and horticultural legacy. And in Penarth, Sue Kent uncovers a rich mix of gardens, personalities and inspiration

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The HSBC Championships. Live coverage of day four of the women's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring singles matches from the second round. Four British players were involved in round two last year, but Emma Raducanu was the only one who progressed, overcoming Slovakian Rebecca ?ramková 6-4, 6-1, while Katie Boulter, Heather Watson and Sonay Kartal all lost out

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges ahead of Friday's final

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Sarajevo, where he traces the fateful last journey in 1914 of the Archduke Franz Ferdinand and his wife, Sophie, which ended in their assassination and led to the outbreak of the First World War. In the old quarter of Bosnia's capital he also admires the rich cultural heritage of the Ottoman Empire

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson casts his eye over the industrious village of Seahouses in Northumberland. He learns how the lethal shoreline shaped its transformation from a sleepy farming outpost to a thriving seaport with a bustling trade in eco-tourism, and uncovers how a family of Scottish entrepreneurs supercharged the herring industry that helped shape the village

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: From Fukushima to California, Hannah explores whether rewilding can help to heal the environmental wounds we have inflicted on our planet.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry takes a drive to the National Highways control centre for the UK's busiest motorway to meet the team that keep the motorways running 24/7

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the annual series charting the fortunes of British wildlife during the changing of the seasons

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the USA Changed Soccer
programme.desc: How soccer in the USA would experience a second coming, with the likes of David Beckham heading to States, after the boom and bust of the 1980s. By the late 1970s, the NASL was continuing to attract the biggest footballing stars in the world, with new innovations making the sport attractive to its American audience. However, the good times didn't last, and the NASL was in a dire financial situation by the mid-1980s, seeing the end of the league, as the money and interest dried up. However, the college system helped fuel the rise of the US Women's National Team, whose World Cup and Olympic triumphs transformed women's football globally, while Beckham's arrival in MLS in 2007 reignited the men's game before Lionel Messi took Inter Miami and MLS to new heights in 2023

programme.title: Britney at the BBC
programme.desc: Her explosive arrival on the music scene aged just 16 made Britney Spears a frequent visitor to some of TV's biggest shows, and this programme captures the best of those performances, with hits including Baby One More Time, Crazy, Overprotected, Toxic and Oops! I Did It Again

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day with Adam Fleming

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Britney Spears was one of the biggest pop stars of her generation, and like many music stars, she was defined by imagery. But she was also the subject of extraordinary exploitation and control. Years of personal and professional turmoil would play out in the frenzied glare of the media spotlight. With the aid of 10 photos, this is the story of a manufactured child star fighting for control over her music, her life and, ultimately, her image

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final week continues with the Chef's Table, in which the amateurs serve food to six leading restaurateurs, including Daniel Clifford, Nieves Barragan and Paul Ainsworth. They are mentored by Aktar Islam of Opheem, a two-Michelin-starred Indian restaurant in Birmingham, and must each serve up a single dish to impress the professionals. As if that were not enough, the finalists then face a cook-off, putting their own twist on a classic dish. And for one hopeful, this will be the end of the road

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and the semi-finalists head to the stadium of Premier League football club Brighton and Hove Albion to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Brighton fan Fatboy Slim and players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby pop in to see how they're getting on, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by designer Shayne Brady to assess the final results

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe feels liberated at a Gods and Monsters party hosted by eccentric local expats Flick and James, while Dan spirals into a dark place back at the villa. News of their separation destabilises each of the friends as the party takes unexpected turns. Drama, starring Jessica Raine

programme.title: This Is BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts sees the warning signs directly spelt out to him at a Merseyside property. Jacqui Joseph meets four friends in Suffolk embarking on their first property project. And Dion Dublin is in Yorkshire where he wants to fill a void

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: Documentary following police officers in Bermuda. A vulnerable elderly man sparks an island-wide search for answers, a roadside stop uncovers a lethal switchblade, and airport officers track suspicious luggage.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From samurai armour to rare wildlife, this episode explores remarkable collections and the experts preserving them. At Snowshill Manor in the Cotswolds, Charles Paget Wade's 22,000 curiosities include rare Japanese armour, with a child's samurai suit restored for exhibition at the British Museum. And at Calke Abbey in Derbyshire, specialists protect historic taxidermy collections

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: Visits to some of the most cavernous places on Earth, including Greenland's bizarre black holes, an ancient forbidden well in the Yemeni desert and a voluminous void in Antarctica

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig joins forces with space journalist Sarah Cruddas to investigate some of the most perplexing UFO encounters of recent times. Here the duo recall the infamous Rendlesham Forest incident of 1980. The sightings occurred just outside RAF Woodbridge, Suffolk, which was used at the time by the United States Air Force

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Lead, Bigfoot Films, and the Lost City in the Gulf of Mexico
programme.desc: The notion that a video leak from the Department of Homeland Security offers more evidence that UFOs really exist. Plus, a consideration of whether one of the world's most respected Bigfoot researchers really did manage to capture footage that proves Sasquatch is no myth

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les's anger over Seth and Ashley's sibling rivalry boils over to the point that he gives them an ultimatum to calm down or be sacked - but they carry on bickering

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les remains in hospital following his collapse, and Seth and Ashley continue manning operations at the pawn shop. However, the bickering continues

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth ignores Ashley's wish to ban a troublesome customer - but his decision threatens to put the shop at risk. Meanwhile, Les commissions a striking masterpiece

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team head to Northwich, Cheshire, where Tim Hogarth buys some 1950s kitsch and Mark Stevens casts his eye over a special bottle of champagne

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick checks out various curious items including a 19th-century Hobbs & Co lock box and an original Woody Woodpecker painting

programme.title: Pawn Stars

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades salvages three items to transform from Altrincham Recycling Centre - mattress springs, an old writing bureau and a broken granite worktop

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: After stopping in Peggy's Cove, Nova Scotia, Scott and Sheldon make a wager to determine who can achieve the best catch in the area

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: The guys come across an old Victorian home while freestyling in Nova Scotia

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: The guys get an out-of-this-world deal on some Star Wars gear and gatecrash a haughty antiques show. Does high-society mingling guarantee top treasures?

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: The guys make a rare Art Deco discovery and spot a weird item in Quebec. They are faced with sky-high prices and reluctant sellers, so they will need to turn on the charm

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick grapples over a decision when a WWE Hall of Fame ring hits the store

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A 1906 silver half dollar that belonged to a passenger on the Titanic is brought into the shop

programme.desc: Enthusiasts scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate ancient man's apparent obsession with building pyramids, and consider whether extraterrestrials may have helped with their construction

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-presenter Sarah Cruddas head to central Scotland, which has its own 'Area 51', known as the Falkirk Triangle, encompassing the now infamous village of Bonnybridge, which claims the highest concentration of the region's UFO activity. They also investigate the alleged abduction of forestry worker Robert Taylor in 1979, the only British UFO incident to have been officially investigated by the police

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: At storage auctions across Canada, high-stakes buyers convene daily. Here, Roy suits up in full army gear to declare war on the buyers in Scarborough

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Wood Green, north London, to help members of the public take on antiques dealers Mark Stevens, Ian Towning, Jan Keyne and David Forde. A gold pocket watch and Albert chain cause plenty of excitement

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy McGrath visits places with an unusual story to tell, kick off with the hidden side of Shropshire

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: An exploration of ancient technologies that seem too advanced for their time, questioning if modern innovations merely reinvented long-lost creations and where those inventions disappeared

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-presenter Sarah Cruddas head to central Scotland, which has its own 'Area 51', known as the Falkirk Triangle, encompassing the now infamous village of Bonnybridge, which claims the highest concentration of the region's UFO activity. They also investigate the alleged abduction of forestry worker Robert Taylor in 1979, the only British UFO incident to have been officially investigated by the police

programme.sub-title: Mutant Mermaid, UFOs vs Nukes and Seamonster Chases a Boat

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In 1997, over the city of Phoenix, Arizona, one of the most spectacular mass UFO sighting of all time took place. Thousands of people witnessed a series of bright orange orbs moving in formation silently overhead. Despite hours of video and photographic evidence this case remains unexplained. Craig and space journalist Sarah Cruddas talk to eyewitness nurse David Parker who provides a vivid description of his close-up encounter with a huge boomerang-shaped UFO that passed directly overhead. They also investigate the response by the authorities in the days following the UFO event
