programme.desc: Martin and Ann invite Paul and his new girlfriend to dinner, but no one expects his estranged wife to turn up as well. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan. First broadcast November 4, 1984

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Peace evades the inmates of Slade Prison, and Fletcher's bid to find a little tranquillity seems destined to fail. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Brian Wilde

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene spots an opportunity to have her old possessions assessed by experts when the Antiques Roadshow arrives in Ludlow, and the farm's staff all share the same idea. However, it turns out that Boycie, with help from Tyler, is the only one to discover a truly valuable item. Fiona Bruce guest stars

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The gang visits 10 Downing Street to campaign against a Japanese takeover - and Mrs Slocombe gets a chance to demonstrate that Britain has women even more formidable than Mrs Thatcher. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and John Inman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour vows that the days of Edie having to carry a dustbin are over, and promises to install a waste disposal unit for her. Meanwhile, a former wartime comrade of Wally's makes an appearance. Michael Aldridge, Thora Hird and Joe Gladwin star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Having witnessed his sister Edie fail several driving tests, Seymour gallantly volunteers to help teach her. However, because he is unable to control a vehicle himself, he needs a qualified motorist to ride along. Michael Aldridge and Thora Hird star

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and the platoon dress as firemen to take part in an initiative exercise, but soon find themselves tackling an all-too-real blaze with only Victorian equipment to rely on. Vintage comedy, starring Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and John Le Mesurier. First broadcast in 1972

programme.title: Red Dwarf

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee has trouble bonding with his new lodger - a surly 14-year-old boy - but Tim has no such problem and discovers they have a lot in common. Meanwhile, Kate tries to teach a carnivorous dog about the joys of eating vegetables. Sitcom, starring Lee Mack, Megan Dodds, Tim Vine and Rupert Simonian

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly is involved in a duel to the death against the Queeg 500 - the super-strict back-up computer that replaced him after a series of disasters. Sci-fi comedy, starring Norman Lovett, Craig Charles, Danny John-Jules and Chris Barrie

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly's new invention hurls the crew into a parallel universe inhabited by their opposites, confirming Lister's darkest fears, and giving Cat a major shock when he meets his counterpart. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Danny John-Jules and Norman Lovett

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the comedy sketch show. Tim Nice-But-Dim takes over an antiques shop, the lovely wobbly randy old ladies go to the launderette, Kevin reaches a landmark birthday and Ben Elton becomes a feminist version of Benny Hill. Plus, Waynetta kicks Wayne out. With Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: An old woman wanders into Lucy's flat and joins Lee on the sofa, but it becomes apparent that she is very confused. He decides to throw her out - only to discover she has a wealthy son who always pays a handsome reward to the person who takes her home. Comedy, starring Lee Mack

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin enters the pub snooker tournament, but is put out when he learns Paul is also a contender. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan. First broadcast November 18, 1984

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber takes up boxing and enters the prison championships. He is hot favourite to win until Fletcher is given the task of fixing the contest and nobbling his cell-mate. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Peter Vaughan

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: First episode of John Sullivan's comedy series, following the lives of Only Fools and Horses characters Boycie and Marlene. Denzil tells Boycie the infamous Driscoll Brothers are after the supergrass who had them jailed - prompting the car salesman to sell his business and move to rural Shropshire. John Challis and Sue Holderness star, with a guest appearance by Paul Barber

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff at Grace Brothers are up in arms when it is decided that those who have reached a certain age are to be made redundant - leaving Mrs Slocombe facing the sack. They are far from appeased when they meet her successor. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot offers his body and Gary fixes up a burglar alarm in forlorn efforts to impress Deborah

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Mr Cholmondley-Warner offers his idiosyncratic views on madness, while Stan and Pam flash their cash in Spain. Kevin refuses to go to bed, Tim gets engaged to a royal and Alf Git meets an old flame at a funeral with explosive results

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot and Gary get serious over a chess contest in which the victor gains an evening of pleasure. Laddish comedy, starring Martin Clunes and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Joe and Chas have been together for nine years, a long time to be truly in love with someone. But how long does it take to properly say goodbye? Darkly comic tale, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with a guest appearance from Sheila Reid

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy becomes a Yellowcoat, but her happiness is spoilt when Alec Foster arrives with bad news. Last-ever episode of the hit comedy, starring Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Peace evades the inmates of Slade Prison, and Fletcher's bid to find a little tranquillity seems destined to fail. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Brian Wilde

programme.desc: Marlene spots an opportunity to have her old possessions assessed by experts when the Antiques Roadshow arrives in Ludlow, and the farm's staff all share the same idea. programme.desc: Richard is forced to think on his feet when he forgets his wedding anniversary and tells Hyacinth he has arranged to have a security alarm fitted as a present. Social-climbing comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and the platoon dress as firemen to take part in an initiative exercise, but soon find themselves tackling an all-too-real blaze with only Victorian equipment to rely on. Vintage comedy, starring Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and John Le Mesurier. First broadcast in 1972

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The crew members wake up to the realisation their memories have been erased - and to make matters worse, some fiend has completed Lister's jigsaw. Comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Norman Lovett and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: A leak from the suspended animation chamber gives the crew a doorway to the past, prompting Lister to race to save lost love Kochanski's life, much to Rimmer's fury. Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Danny John-Jules, Norman Lovett and Clare Grogan star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee has trouble bonding with his new lodger - a surly 14-year-old boy - but Tim has no such problem and discovers they have a lot in common. Meanwhile, Kate tries to teach a carnivorous dog about the joys of eating vegetables. Sitcom, starring Lee Mack, Megan Dodds, Tim Vine and Rupert Simonian

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly is involved in a duel to the death against the Queeg 500 - the super-strict back-up computer that replaced him after a series of disasters. Sci-fi comedy, starring Norman Lovett, Craig Charles, Danny John-Jules and Chris Barrie

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly's new invention hurls the crew into a parallel universe inhabited by their opposites, confirming Lister's darkest fears, and giving Cat a major shock when he meets his counterpart. Sci-fi comedy, starring Chris Barrie, Craig Charles, Danny John-Jules and Norman Lovett

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the popular funnyman, featuring Kevin and Perry, Wayne and Waynetta Slob and the Self-Righteous Brothers. With special guests Desmond Lynam, Jimmy Hill and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dorothy welcomes Gary's suggestion of an open relationship, while Dermot continues to woo Deborah

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Comedy sketches with the popular funnyman, featuring Kevin and Perry, Wayne and Waynetta Slob and the Self-Righteous Brothers. With special guests Desmond Lynam, Jimmy Hill and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is upset that Trevor has decided to work for a mission project in Africa, and resolves to give him an unforgettable send-off to remind him of the loving family he has in Ireland. Cathy's family counselling session descends into chaos, Buster and Dermot land themselves in trouble because of a circus lion, and neighbour Winnie is delighted that her husband's libido has returned. Comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Peggy's piano skills upset Ted's delicate musical ear, so he challenges the royal marines to race over their assault course. Comedy, starring Paul Shane, Kenneth Connor and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Clive and Gladys cancel their wedding, but it remains to be seen whether April will marry Spike. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin enters the pub snooker tournament, but is put out when he learns Paul is also a contender. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan. First broadcast November 18, 1984

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber takes up boxing and enters the prison championships. He is hot favourite to win until Fletcher is given the task of fixing the contest and nobbling his cell-mate. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Peter Vaughan

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: First episode of John Sullivan's comedy series, following the lives of Only Fools and Horses characters Boycie and Marlene. Denzil tells Boycie the infamous Driscoll Brothers are after the supergrass who had them jailed - prompting the car salesman to sell his business and move to rural Shropshire. John Challis and Sue Holderness star, with a guest appearance by Paul Barber

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff at Grace Brothers are up in arms when it is decided that those who have reached a certain age are to be made redundant - leaving Mrs Slocombe facing the sack. They are far from appeased when they meet her successor. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo speculates why blood rushes to the head when upside down but not to the feet while standing up, so Seymour sets up an experiment to demonstrate the way the body works. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Michael Aldridge

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Nikki Fox and Matt Allwright hunt for antiques in Kent alongside expert guides Natasha Raskin Sharp and David Harper. As they seek out items to sell at auction, the rookie antiques dealers find the time to learn about William Morris's manifesto to save ancient buildings, and discover the connection between Kentish ragstone and the early Victorian dinosaur hunters

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Made in Chelsea's Sam Thompson takes to the Cotswolds B-roads with his best friend, Pete Wicks from The Only Way is Essex. Under the supervision of experts Margie Cooper and Raj Bisram, these reality TV star rivals go head-to-head as they swap on-screen antics for auction-worthy antiques

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Celebrity PR Edina works from home and struggles to cope after Saffy's departure. Matters come to a head when client Emma Bunton arrives for a meeting, and is horrified at the state of the house, but luckily Saffy turns up and lets slip a big secret that might just render her mother speechless. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy's pregnancy sends her mum off the deep end, bringing nightmares about becoming a grandmother, her torture compounded by the taunting midwife. Luckily, Eddie's new celebrity book club started with Patsy takes her mind off her troubles, as Mariella Frostrup promises to bring her famous friends

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy resents Saffy's new close relationship with her mother, but is soothed by an encounter with guest star Minnie Driver. Edina's delight at the prospect of having a mixed-race grandchild is topped by her installation of a panic room, complete with champagne and a surveillance system

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Today's service challenge is in Melbourne's Spotswood, a huge park filled with street food outlets. Every team must create two dishes, surf and turf, each to be sold for $10

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of Manchester couple Rebecca and Steve, whose life was changed for ever when they found out they were expecting triplets. Two years on, Rebecca is becoming depressed by the state of their small, damp, dilapidated house, which is unsafe for the children. The builders have just nine days to bring the property up to date and give this tired mum and dad the space they need for their active offspring. Luckily, local tradespeople and other members of the community turn out in force to help

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Spalding, Lincolnshire, to meet Dakota Read and her family. The eight-year-old was born with quadriplegic cerebral palsy and her home needs to be made more wheelchair-friendly. Enter the DIY SOS builders, who set to work on designer Gabrielle Blackman's vision, although with just nine days to complete the task, they'll need help. Luckily, the local tradespeople are also out in force, giving their time and their skills for free

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays' home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Nikki Fox and Matt Allwright hunt for antiques in Kent alongside expert guides Natasha Raskin Sharp and David Harper. As they seek out items to sell at auction, the rookie antiques dealers find the time to learn about William Morris's manifesto to save ancient buildings, and discover the connection between Kentish ragstone and the early Victorian dinosaur hunters

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Broadcasters Mark Radcliffe and Edith Bowman put down their mics and battle it out to pick up some profit-making collectables and curios. Antiques experts Paul Martin and Natasha Raskin Sharp are along for the ride as they roar around the roads of the Midlands in an iconic DeLorean and a classy-looking Rover

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A rescue special, featuring the amazing stories - old and new - of some of the animals for whom Noel has provided extraordinary and life-saving treatment since The Supervet began in 2014, and the families at the centre of their inspiring stories. Animals featured include rescue whippet Wobble, who at only a year old in 2015 was wobbling due to a compression fracture in her neck and German shepherd rescue Bran, who first came to Fitzpatricks in 2019 and received an innovative custom total hip replacement - the first of its kind for a three-legged dog

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Bedlington terrier, who has suffered a broken front leg three times in the last four years, is referred to Noel with another fracture in her leg. Elsewhere, beloved fox red Labrador Jethro has arthritic elbows, which have left him unable to walk properly and are causing him great pain. Noel uses a tiny camera to take a closer look at the joint damage and determine the best course of action

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family
programme.desc: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, who's terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.desc: As the FBI put pressure on Harlee, she refuses to provide any incriminating evidence on Matt - but she does mention teammate Tess's suspicious role in a murder investigation

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.sub-title: Who Can Tell Me Who Am I?
programme.desc: Wozniak realises Donny has been withholding information, while Harlee's attempt to feign co-operation with FBI handler Stahl backfires when she leads him to incriminating evidence. Loman crumbles under the guilt of shooting an unarmed suspect

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Lucy Porter and Chaser Jenny Ryan visit antique shops in south Wales. On hand to help are experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore. An alcohol-themed spending spree sees both celebrity trippers purchasing items once used for marketing boozy products. However, they buy classy objects too, including something originating from Buckingham Palace and some quite pricy oak library steps

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Broadcasters Mark Radcliffe and Edith Bowman put down their mics and battle it out to pick up some profit-making collectables and curios. Antiques experts Paul Martin and Natasha Raskin Sharp are along for the ride as they roar around the roads of the Midlands in an iconic DeLorean and a classy-looking Rover

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie and Patsy take a weekend hunting trip in the country. Back home, Saffy has a baby shower with her friends, but one of them develops an unhealthy fixation. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley, with guest appearances by Emma Bunton and Clarissa Dickson Wright

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina finds herself on the horns of a dilemma - Patsy is presenting the fashion event of the year at the Royal Albert Hall, but Saffy has gone unexpectedly into labour, leaving her mother torn between love and duty

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: A new boyfriend boasting a selection of lost Beatles recordings makes Eddie think she's hit the big time, but her plans to marry him and invite an elite group of producers to hear the tapes are dashed in spectacular fashion. Elton John and Robert Lindsay guest star

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Immunity is up for grabs, as well as a $10,000 prize! Plus, the winning contestant gets to take part in a cook-off against professional chef Sergio Perera

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Hereford couple Colin and Clare Gibbons, whose life together fell apart in 2012 when their eldest son Christopher was diagnosed with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, one of the most severe forms of the condition. Despite having a lovely house, it needs adapting to Christopher's needs for both now and in the future, so the DIY SOS team - bolstered by dozens of local tradespeople - descend on the property to add a two-storey extension, with designer Julia Kendell on hand to ensure they stick to her vision while adding a few finishing touches of her own

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Lucy Porter and Chaser Jenny Ryan visit antique shops in south Wales. On hand to help are experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore. An alcohol-themed spending spree sees both celebrity trippers purchasing items once used for marketing boozy products. However, they buy classy objects too, including something originating from Buckingham Palace and some quite pricy oak library steps

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Maine Coon cat is brought into the practice with a fractured hip joint, which Noel determines is caused by a genetic disease that has made the heads of both of her femur bones degenerate. After a very challenging process of weighing up the pros and cons of surgery, the owners opt for bilateral total hip replacement.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Eight-month-old Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in as a late-night emergency after being hit by a car, while four-year-old bull mastiff Bayleigh is referred to Noel with a suspicious and painful lump near her ankle. Cassie and Ashley thought their only option was to have their cat's leg amputated, but a chance encounter with Noel has given them hope that it could possibly be saved.

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Hugo and the Ice Cream Pups
programme.desc: Pete meets some newborn pups fighting to survive, and helps teach Hugo, a Cane Corso rescued from quarantine at Dover, how to be a dog

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Warrington, Cheshire, to meet electrician Dave McCartney, a healthy man until he had a stroke at work that has left him partially paralysed. Now he is forced to sleep in a hospital bed in his kitchen - but it won't be for much longer, as his old workmates team up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to adapt his home to his needs, with designer Charlie Luxton on hand to keep the boys in check

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Orpington, south-east London, to meet hospital worker Jason Sulong, who was involved in a motorcycle accident that left him partially paralysed. Now he is unable to climb the stairs in his home and has difficulty accessing his bathroom and washing facilities, so designer Gabrielle Blackman teams up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to work to adapt his home to his needs over the course of just nine days

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A film crew comes to Button House to shoot a reconstruction of an assassination plot for a historical documentary - a scheme which may have involved one of the spirits. Mike persuades Alison to give an interview about the old house in the hope of drumming up some publicity, forcing her to turn to Thomas for advice on the art of public speaking

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House, with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is confused by his feelings for this strange arrival, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy is persuaded to allow Patsy and Eddie to babysit for the day, allowing her to have some romantic time with John - unaware the pair have an ulterior motive involving a fashion shoot and an outrageous designer. Fern Britton and Jean-Paul Gaultier guest star

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The first of the final two episodes of the sitcom sees the unlucky-in-love shop owner having received two proposals of marriage - one from dreamboat chef Gary and the other from new boyfriend Mike. Who will she choose, if anyone? And if she does make a decision, could she actually cope with the pressures of being a wife? Tom Ellis and Bo Poraj co-star as the rivals for her affections, with Patricia Hodge, Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The very last episode of the hit sitcom sees the shopkeeper making one final attempt to accept herself and find contentment. Will she ever be truly happy? Miranda Hart stars, with Patricia Hodge, Tom Ellis, Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips, plus guest appearances from Mark Heap, Liza Tarbuck and Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: It's the final day of Big Week and judge Mel reveals that today's challenge is an overnight cook

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Today in Stoke, paramedics Mick and Rachel help a young man who has hurt his back. In Dudley, paramedics help a 74-year-old who had a shock from his internal defibrillator

Antiques experts Paul Martin and Natasha Raskin Sharp are along for the ride as they roar around the roads of the Midlands in an iconic DeLorean and a classy-looking Rover

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A rescue special, featuring the amazing stories - old and new - of some of the animals for whom Noel has provided extraordinary and life-saving treatment since The Supervet began in 2014, and the families at the centre of their inspiring stories. Animals featured include rescue whippet Wobble, who at only a year old in 2015 was wobbling due to a compression fracture in her neck and German shepherd rescue Bran, who first came to Fitzpatricks in 2019 and received an innovative custom total hip replacement - the first of its kind for a three-legged dog

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Bedlington terrier, who has suffered a broken front leg three times in the last four years, is referred to Noel with another fracture in her leg. Elsewhere, beloved fox red Labrador Jethro has arthritic elbows, which have left him unable to walk properly and are causing him great pain. Noel uses a tiny camera to take a closer look at the joint damage and determine the best course of action

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of Manchester couple Rebecca and Steve, whose life was changed for ever when they found out they were expecting triplets. Two years on, Rebecca is becoming depressed by the state of their small, damp, dilapidated house, which is unsafe for the children. The builders have just nine days to bring the property up to date and give this tired mum and dad the space they need for their active offspring. Luckily, local tradespeople and other members of the community turn out in force to help

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Spalding, Lincolnshire, to meet Dakota Read and her family. The eight-year-old was born with quadriplegic cerebral palsy and her home needs to be made more wheelchair-friendly. Enter the DIY SOS builders, who set to work on designer Gabrielle Blackman's vision, although with just nine days to complete the task, they'll need help. Luckily, the local tradespeople are also out in force, giving their time and their skills for free

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Terri, Esme and the Chi Family
programme.desc: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, who's terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.desc: As the FBI put pressure on Harlee, she refuses to provide any incriminating evidence on Matt - but she does mention teammate Tess's suspicious role in a murder investigation

programme.title: Shades of Blue
programme.sub-title: Who Can Tell Me Who Am I?
programme.desc: Wozniak realises Donny has been withholding information, while Harlee's attempt to feign co-operation with FBI handler Stahl backfires when she leads him to incriminating evidence. Loman crumbles under the guilt of shooting an unarmed suspect

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Nikki Fox and Matt Allwright hunt for antiques in Kent alongside expert guides Natasha Raskin Sharp and David Harper. As they seek out items to sell at auction, the rookie antiques dealers find the time to learn about William Morris's manifesto to save ancient buildings, and discover the connection between Kentish ragstone and the early Victorian dinosaur hunters

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Eddie and Patsy take a weekend hunting trip in the country. Back home, Saffy has a baby shower with her friends, but one of them develops an unhealthy fixation. Comedy, starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley, with guest appearances by Emma Bunton and Clarissa Dickson Wright

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina finds herself on the horns of a dilemma - Patsy is presenting the fashion event of the year at the Royal Albert Hall, but Saffy has gone unexpectedly into labour, leaving her mother torn between love and duty

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: A new boyfriend boasting a selection of lost Beatles recordings makes Eddie think she's hit the big time, but her plans to marry him and invite an elite group of producers to hear the tapes are dashed in spectacular fashion. Elton John and Robert Lindsay guest star

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Immunity is up for grabs, as well as a $10,000 prize! Plus, the winning contestant gets to take part in a cook-off against professional chef Sergio Perera

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Hereford couple Colin and Clare Gibbons, whose life together fell apart in 2012 when their eldest son Christopher was diagnosed with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, one of the most severe forms of the condition. Despite having a lovely house, it needs adapting to Christopher's needs for both now and in the future, so the DIY SOS team - bolstered by dozens of local tradespeople - descend on the property to add a two-storey extension, with designer Julia Kendell on hand to ensure they stick to her vision while adding a few finishing touches of her own

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Lucy Porter and Chaser Jenny Ryan visit antique shops in south Wales. On hand to help are experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore. An alcohol-themed spending spree sees both celebrity trippers purchasing items once used for marketing boozy products. However, they buy classy objects too, including something originating from Buckingham Palace and some quite pricy oak library steps

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone and saxophonist YolanDa Brown hunt for items in Edinburgh and Fife, with experts David Harper and Raj Bisram on hand to advise. Among the items of note are novelty sugar tongs, a Native American drum and a rare green pig

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Maine Coon cat is brought into the practice with a fractured hip joint, which Noel determines is caused by a genetic disease that has made the heads of both of her femur bones degenerate. After a very challenging process of weighing up the pros and cons of surgery, the owners opt for bilateral total hip replacement.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Eight-month-old Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in as a late-night emergency after being hit by a car, while four-year-old bull mastiff Bayleigh is referred to Noel with a suspicious and painful lump near her ankle. Cassie and Ashley thought their only option was to have their cat's leg amputated, but a chance encounter with Noel has given them hope that it could possibly be saved.

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Hugo and the Ice Cream Pups
programme.desc: Pete meets some newborn pups fighting to survive, and helps teach Hugo, a Cane Corso rescued from quarantine at Dover, how to be a dog

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Warrington, Cheshire, to meet electrician Dave McCartney, a healthy man until he had a stroke at work that has left him partially paralysed. Now he is forced to sleep in a hospital bed in his kitchen - but it won't be for much longer, as his old workmates team up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to adapt his home to his needs, with designer Charlie Luxton on hand to keep the boys in check

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Orpington, south-east London, to meet hospital worker Jason Sulong, who was involved in a motorcycle accident that left him partially paralysed. Now he is unable to climb the stairs in his home and has difficulty accessing his bathroom and washing facilities, so designer Gabrielle Blackman teams up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to work to adapt his home to his needs over the course of just nine days

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Lucy Porter and Chaser Jenny Ryan visit antique shops in south Wales. On hand to help are experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore. An alcohol-themed spending spree sees both celebrity trippers purchasing items once used for marketing boozy products. However, they buy classy objects too, including something originating from Buckingham Palace and some quite pricy oak library steps

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike camp in the grounds while the house is being fumigated for woodworm, and become competitive over who is the better camper. Pat and the Captain embrace the chance to spend some time in the great outdoors again, but Mary is terrified that the 'woodworm men' intend to rid the house of its ghostly residents and resolves to scare them away

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Three Swedish Death Cleaners - an organizer, a designer and a psychologist - travel across America to help people face mortality and organise their homes and lives before it's too late. The trio help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels, let go of the past and move forward

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Saffy is persuaded to allow Patsy and Eddie to babysit for the day, allowing her to have some romantic time with John - unaware the pair have an ulterior motive involving a fashion shoot and an outrageous designer. Fern Britton and Jean-Paul Gaultier guest star

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The first of the final two episodes of the sitcom sees the unlucky-in-love shop owner having received two proposals of marriage - one from dreamboat chef Gary and the other from new boyfriend Mike. Who will she choose, if anyone? And if she does make a decision, could she actually cope with the pressures of being a wife? Tom Ellis and Bo Poraj co-star as the rivals for her affections, with Patricia Hodge, Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: The very last episode of the hit sitcom sees the shopkeeper making one final attempt to accept herself and find contentment. Will she ever be truly happy? Miranda Hart stars, with Patricia Hodge, Tom Ellis, Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips, plus guest appearances from Mark Heap, Liza Tarbuck and Joe Wilkinson

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: It's the final day of Big Week and judge Mel reveals that today's challenge is an overnight cook

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Today in Stoke, paramedics Mick and Rachel help a young man who has hurt his back. In Dudley, paramedics help a 74-year-old who had a shock from his internal defibrillator

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine that's seen better days

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Speed freak Guy Martin embarks on a mission to fly using muscle power alone, by building the world's fastest human-powered aircraft. He heads to Southampton University where, in 1961, Derek Piggot became the first man to fly under his own power. Guy teams up with a group of engineers from the same university to build a plane that Guy will ride like a bicycle - will he manage to fly into the record books?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer heads to Colorado where he hopes to win one of America's oldest races - the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb. In this annual contest, known as the Race to the Clouds, riders compete against the clock on a gruelling mountainside track that finishes more than 13,000ft above sea level. To learn the skills required in hill-climb events, Guy visits the birthplace of the sport - Shelsley Walsh in Worcestershire. Racing up the course in an F1-style car, he realises sheer horsepower and driving precision are vital, but he must also take a high-altitude fitness test in a hypoxic chamber to prepare for the challenge ahead

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A secret weapons program in the heart of Austria that could have ended badly for the Allies - and may have indicated nuclear plans being concocted by Hitler

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team heads to Somerleyton Hall, near Lowestoft, where the objects up for valuation include a bracelet given to Queen Victoria and a picture painted by a suffragette artist. The experts also investigate the origins of an intriguing box of paints that is said to have belonged to John Constable

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the Steam Museum in Swindon, where objects examined by the experts include one of the rarest pieces of silver seen on the show and a valuable painting that was about to be thrown away. Among the more bizarre sights are uneaten slices of royal wedding cakes dating back to Queen Victoria, and hundreds of rail tickets for axed stations

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.desc: The A4 LNER Pacific Locomotive, Sir Nigel Gresley, must be hauled 46 miles at 30mph from Crewe to Bridgnorth

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle that's ripe for restoration

programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer leaves his comfort zone as he attempts to set a world speed record for a hovercraft. These machines are notoriously difficult to control, so to learn the basic skills needed he's put through his paces by Royal Marines in a military training operation. To achieve his goal, Guy will need to stay over 86mph for a kilometre, but at these speeds, because of the hovercraft's inherent instability, even the most experienced pilots can lose control - and the worsening weather makes things much more challenging

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike fan takes on the challenge of building the world's fastest soapbox racer in an attempt to set a new speed record using just the pull of gravity and a tarmac road. To get to grips with going blisteringly fast on a public road, Guy tries his hand at street luge with former global champion Helene Schmit, before joining his team of engineers to explore the science of rolling resistance and the importance of tyre pressure. Armed with what they believe to be the perfect formula, they produce a unique soapbox cart inspired by Mother Nature and, after training with the Team GB women's bobsled pair, confidence is high - but are disaster and mayhem just around the corner?

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise for Derek in Hull - an MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a rare Ford Zodiac Farnham Estate, while Dave heads for an isolated tent in the Midlands to collect a Triumph Toledo

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Guided missiles, the Wasserfall, and the war-changing V2 were dreamed up and tested in Peenemunde, a German seaside city suddenly overtaken by Nazi elite

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Brighton College, where objects up for valuation include a medal awarded to a teenage veteran of the Battle of Trafalgar, a silver box given to a family by US president John F Kennedy shortly before his assassination, and one of the largest, rarest and most valuable pieces of Clarice Cliff pottery ever seen on the programme

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Brighton College, where objects up for valuation include a cup and saucer purchased at a car boot sale, a portrait of a pig by a noted artist, and a collection of items found in a safe that once belonged to Agatha Christie

programme.desc: The team take on one of Derek's favourites - an Austin A35 - but its age ensures some hefty bills. Their second project is a quick flip - a 1999 BMW Z3

programme.desc: Dave travels to Bedale to extract a barn find - a 1984 Manta GTE which hasn't seen the light of day in thirty years

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Guy Martin attempts to break the motorcycle land speed record, which currently stands at 376mph. He travels to Bonneville Salt Flats in Nevada, where he must learn to ride a unique jet-propelled bike which has two engines and requires a completely different driving technique to any vehicle he has previously encountered

programme.title: Our Guy in Colombia
programme.desc: Part one of two. Guy Martin explores Colombia's violent underworld, undergoing the same kidnap training that politicians go through and visiting a bullet-proof clothing factory. To find out more about the cocaine trade, which is a root cause of many of Colombia's issues, Guy loads a motorcycle into the back of a cargo plane and flies into the Amazon, to an airfield cut into the jungle by notorious drugs kingpin Pablo Escobar

programme.desc: Speed freak Guy Martin embarks on a mission to fly using muscle power alone, by building the world's fastest human-powered aircraft. He heads to Southampton University where, in 1961, Derek Piggot became the first man to fly under his own power. Guy teams up with a group of engineers from the same university to build a plane that Guy will ride like a bicycle - will he manage to fly into the record books?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike racer takes part in four more speed-based challenges and gets his hands dirty in unique engineering projects to discover what makes things go fast. In the first programme, he finds out how far it's possible to travel on a bicycle during 24 hours of non-stop pedalling. With the help of bike experts Miles Kingsbury and Mike Burrows he builds a tandem, and pairs up with his friend, endurance expert Jason Miles, to see how far they can push their bodies. In the build-up to the final record attempt at the historic Goodwood Motor Circuit, Guy also learns about the science of cooking for athletes with nutritionist Nigel Mitchell, who instructs him to lose five kilos and recommends a new diet regimen

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer heads to Colorado where he hopes to win one of America's oldest races - the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb. In this annual contest, known as the Race to the Clouds, riders compete against the clock on a gruelling mountainside track that finishes more than 13,000ft above sea level. To learn the skills required in hill-climb events, Guy visits the birthplace of the sport - Shelsley Walsh in Worcestershire. Racing up the course in an F1-style car, he realises sheer horsepower and driving precision are vital, but he must also take a high-altitude fitness test in a hypoxic chamber to prepare for the challenge ahead

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A secret weapons program in the heart of Austria that could have ended badly for the Allies - and may have indicated nuclear plans being concocted by Hitler

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team heads to Somerleyton Hall, near Lowestoft, where the objects up for valuation include a bracelet given to Queen Victoria and a picture painted by a suffragette artist. The experts also investigate the origins of an intriguing box of paints that is said to have belonged to John Constable

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the Steam Museum in Swindon, where objects examined by the experts include one of the rarest pieces of silver seen on the show and a valuable painting that was about to be thrown away. Among the more bizarre sights are uneaten slices of royal wedding cakes dating back to Queen Victoria, and hundreds of rail tickets for axed stations

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorcycle racer leaves his comfort zone as he attempts to set a world speed record for a hovercraft. These machines are notoriously difficult to control, so to learn the basic skills needed he's put through his paces by Royal Marines in a military training operation. To achieve his goal, Guy will need to stay over 86mph for a kilometre, but at these speeds, because of the hovercraft's inherent instability, even the most experienced pilots can lose control - and the worsening weather makes things much more challenging

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: The motorbike fan takes on the challenge of building the world's fastest soapbox racer in an attempt to set a new speed record using just the pull of gravity and a tarmac road. To get to grips with going blisteringly fast on a public road, Guy tries his hand at street luge with former global champion Helene Schmit, before joining his team of engineers to explore the science of rolling resistance and the importance of tyre pressure. Armed with what they believe to be the perfect formula, they produce a unique soapbox cart inspired by Mother Nature and, after training with the Team GB women's bobsled pair, confidence is high - but are disaster and mayhem just around the corner?

programme.desc: Documentary examining the sharp rise in fatalities on the nation's roads, hearing from families of some of those who have died, who speak candidly about the lives lost, the ongoing grief, and their hopes that meaningful change can come from their experiences. The film investigates growing criticism of Ireland's road safety system, exploring concerns around lengthy delays in driving tests, the high number of learner drivers on the road, and whether current Essential Driver Training (EDT) requirements adequately reflect increasingly complex road conditions

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headlines from 1998

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories from 2001, the year when two planes hit the Twin Towers in New York. It also saw the foot-and-mouth outbreak and Eamon Dunphy turn his hand to TV presenting

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A London policeman and his doctor wife encounter hostility and suspicion when they move to a close-knit Yorkshire community, but feuding mods and rockers, a mysterious prowler and a budgie-killer mean the couple soon find themselves in the thick of village life. First-ever episode of the popular Sixties-set drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack, Derek Fowlds and Bill Maynard

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The naked bodies of three men are found placed in the shape of a triangle, leading Jack to suspect the work of a satanic cult. Meanwhile, press baron James Callum's plans to make his mark on Denton are hindered by an investigation into his lover's death in a houseboat fire - and the suspicious demise of his former girlfriend may be the key to unmasking the killer. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Adrian Lukis, Sarah Matravers and Paul Shane

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner make dishes perfect for picnics, including simple jam and treacle mini tarts, salmon and cucumber pinwheels, and sausage rolls. Finally, they bring together beautiful spices with a take on simple Bombay potatoes, a chickpea dahl and oven-baked samosas

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover is back and bigger than ever on Quest Red. Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home mkeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes. In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: A fire alarm causes Paddington Station to be evacuated in rush hour, and soaring temperatures sees disruption to the network across the busy Bank Holiday weekend

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A flamboyant photographer and his models descend on the village, while Nick is called on to sort out a dispute at a hotel _ and finds himself looking down the barrel of a gun. Drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts use cutting-edge tech and bold engineering to build a record-breaking bridge that spans a deadly chasm in a remote section of the Himalayas. When completed, it will be the world's tallest railway bridge, towering almost 1,200 feet high

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson meets novice hoteliers Elly Koopman and Rob Farrow and assesses their nine-bedroom Haven Hotel in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk. Bought only four months prior to Ruth's arrival, the establishment has left the pair out of their depth and desperately in need of a crash course in running a hotel

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke bring unloved gardens back to life in a single day using clever design, imaginative ideas and recycling or re-use of existing plants and materials. They begin by travelling to Blackpool to transform Paul and Sarah's boggy, waterlogged plot

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The train on which Marian is travelling crashes, trapping her in the wreckage. Although she cannot escape, she's free to move around the carriage to help those injured, assisted by fellow passenger Dr Nick Burnett - who turns out to be the Royal's new locum. Back at the hospital, a shortage of staff puts the casualty department under pressure when the crash victims begin to arrive. Damian O'Hare joins the cast, and Gerard Horan guest stars

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is assigned to investigate when a chocolate factory owner is murdered during a fox hunt. The case leads him to the sleepy Oxfordshire village of Chigton Green, and is soon complicated by the fatal shooting of a local couple with an apparent connection to the first victim. Endeavour uncovers a vicious campaign of gossip and rumour, and meets a local woman who inspires him to consider his future

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An old pal of Greengrass's turns up with a money-making scheme, but the pair are soon out of their depth and Nick is forced to go undercover to sort it out. Meanwhile, Ventress tries to organise a marquee for his daughter's wedding - with disappointing results. Police drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: Live International Football
programme.desc: England v Costa Rica (Kick-off 9.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Inter&Co Stadium in Orlando, Florida. This is England's final warm-up game before they get their World Cup campaign under way against Croatia in Dallas on 17th June. They will be expecting a morale-boosting victory here against a team that have failed to qualify for the finals for the first time since 2010, and they prevailed 2-0 when the sides last met at Elland Road in 2018 thanks to goals by Marcus Rashford and Danny Welbeck

programme.desc: A former government agent is shocked to discover his arch-enemy has infiltrated a gang of extreme environmentalists - and is bent on contaminating the world with a deadly nerve gas unless he's paid a ransom. Thriller, starring Jerome Ehlers, Antonio Sabato Jnr, Kimberly Davies and Kate Beahan

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick pursues a moorland flasher and drunken hikers, while Kate suffers abuse at the hands of a bullying husband. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack investigates the abduction of a bus driver and his conductor and an unrelated murder of a children's entertainer, but is frustrated to find himself working alongside a former colleague who got him suspended by making a complaint about his handling of a case. Long-running crime drama, with David Jason, and co-starring Cherie Lunghi and John Lyons

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen

programme.desc: Nick admits some of Elsinby's homeless people to the hospital, despite Carnegie'a disapproval. Ormerod's intention to keep Dr Weatherill on light duties is scuppered when she attends an emergency call on the coastal road, where a woman in labour is trapped in a car. Meanwhile, Matron is surprised by the identity of the new student nurse, and a gossipy patient reveals the whereabouts of Ken. James Greene and Judith Barker guest star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a librarian, and suspicion immediately falls on two professors connected to each other by a seemingly innocent college bequest. As Endeavour and Thursday dig deeper, they uncover a web of professional and personal rivalries, and a series of broken promises with a grim connection to the Holocaust. There are further casualties in the recent series of heroin-related deaths, and Strange is convinced that a larger criminal conspiracy is at work as evidence emerges of a link to the murder of George Fancy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A former acquaintance of Nick's arrives from London and raises suspicions about a string of violent robberies. Meanwhile, Nick's mum takes a room at the Aidensfield Arms and begins helping out behind the bar. Drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: ER

programme.title: The Day Diana Died
programme.desc: Piecing together the critical hours following the news of the Princess of Wales' death and examining key decisions made by the Royal Household, newsrooms and Tony Blair's government. This programme looks at the moment the Queen was informed about Diana's death in Paris, and follows the return of her body to British soil. Featuring contributions from Tina Brown, Colin Tebbutt, Dickie Arbiter and Ian Hislop

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Archie scams Caleb, while Gabby's healthy-eating obsession takes a darker tone and Graham urges Kyle to talk to Cain

programme.desc: The pressures of work take their toll on Jo and she turns to drink for comfort. Kirsty refuses to get involved, leaving Danny to pick up the pieces when Jo reacts badly to his offer of support. Ashika makes some big decisions both on and off the campaign trail, and Scott is headhunted to help the flagging Labour candidate in Sedley

programme.title: Safe Harbour
programme.desc: As Marco's plans start showing results, Toby wants in. However, when things take a dark turn, Sloane asks Kieran to step in. Drama, starring Alfie Allen, Charlie Murphy and Martijn Lakemeier

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Tragedy casts a dark shadow over the idyllic landscape of Aidensfield when Nick receives an anonymous tip-off about a local shopkeeper's unsavoury antics. Meanwhile, Greengrass finds himself in trouble courtesy of the village gossip-mongers. Sixties-based rural drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Inspector Jack Frost reopens an unsolved murder case when the remains of a 14-year-old girl who went missing 20 years previously are discovered on a housing development. With the lead suspect back on the streets after serving a prison sentence for the murder of the teenager's best friend, the detective becomes more determined than ever to put the case to rest. Meanwhile, a late-night prank results in the death of a local businessman. Starring David Jason, Keith Barron and Paula Wilcox

Each player's number is also the total of questions they must answer correctly in the final game against the clock

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Live Athletics: Diamond League
programme.desc: The Bislett Games. Coverage of the sixth meeting of the campaign, held at Bislett Stadium in Oslo, Norway. George Mills set a new British record of 12:46.59 in the 5,000m here last year, but still had to settle for fourth place behind American victor Nico Young and Ethiopians Biniam Mehary and Kuma Girma, There was also a win for Young's compatriot Isabella Whittaker in the 400m, who beat home favourite Henriette Jaeger into second place, with Team GB's Amber Anning third and Ireland's Rhasidat Adeleke fourth
